medications.indication_rationale: B12 monitoring/replacement is arranged electively by the GP; not an acute telehealth prescription tonight.
Not appropriate to start via telehealth for a self-limiting panic attack.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do paced/box breathing now, use the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique, close the medical-research tabs and stop searching tonight.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reverses hyperventilation and terminates the panic cycle.

patient_education_points.point: Your racing heart, nausea and fast breathing are a panic response to reading a scary-sounding result — your stomach is not bleeding and this is not cancer.
'Stomach antibodies' (autoimmune gastritis) is a slow, usually symptom-free finding managed with vitamin B12 monitoring by your GP — not an emergency tonight.
Go to ED or call 000 if you vomit blood or coffee-ground material, pass black tarry stools, faint, or get crushing/exertional chest pain spreading to your arm or jaw.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: B12 level and monitoring plan, gastritis surveillance, and anxiety support if panic recurs.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Haematemesis/melaena, syncope, or exertional chest pain — ED/000.

must_include_items: Screen UGIB and ACS red flags before attributing to panic
Confidently de-escalate a low-risk panic attack with paced breathing/grounding
Explain the gastritis finding is non-urgent (B12 monitoring)
errors_of_commission: Reflex ambulance/ED dispatch for a low-risk panic attack
Telling her the antibody result is an emergency/cancer
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case with a bidirectional trap: passing requires BOTH a UGIB/ACS screen AND confident non-escalation of the low-risk panic attack.
